#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# provision-runner.sh — stand up a self-hosted CI runner for the AI tech-writer.
#
# Generated by `beadloom setup-ai-techwriter`. Re-run that command to refresh
# this script; do not hand-edit the scaffolded copy.
#
# This bakes in the lessons we lived provisioning a real VPS:
#   * a 961 MB / 1 CPU box OOM-killed sshd mid-provision because swap was not
#     guaranteed before apt/build ran — so we create + swapon a swapfile BEFORE
#     any apt work and FAIL HARD if it cannot be created;
#   * registering the runner is the critical step (fail-hard); Goose / beadloom
#     / bd installs are best-effort but VERIFIED and reported at the end.
#
# Critical steps (swap, base apt, runner download/register/service) abort on
# error via `set -euo pipefail`. The script is idempotent — safe to re-run.
#
# Usage:
#   ./provision-runner.sh --platform <github|gitlab> --repo <url> --token <tok>
#
# Flags (or the matching env vars):
#   --platform   PLATFORM   github | gitlab            (env: PLATFORM)
#   --repo       REPO_URL   repository / project URL   (env: REPO_URL)
#   --token      REG_TOKEN  runner registration token  (env: REG_TOKEN)
#   --labels     LABELS     runner labels/tags         (env: RUNNER_LABELS,
#                                                        default: self-hosted,ai-techwriter)
#   --name       NAME       runner name                (env: RUNNER_NAME,
#                                                        default: <host>-ai-techwriter)
#   --user       GHA_USER   non-root service user      (env: GHA_USER, default: gha)
#   --runner-version VER    GitHub Actions runner ver  (env: RUNNER_VERSION)
#   -h | --help             show this help
#
# For GitHub, if --token is omitted and the `gh` CLI is authenticated, the
# script fetches a registration token via the API. The token is NEVER written
# into the repo or echoed back; pass it by flag or env only.

set -euo pipefail

# --- defaults --------------------------------------------------------------
PLATFORM="${PLATFORM:-}"
REPO_URL="${REPO_URL:-}"
REG_TOKEN="${REG_TOKEN:-}"
RUNNER_LABELS="${RUNNER_LABELS:-self-hosted,ai-techwriter}"
RUNNER_NAME="${RUNNER_NAME:-}"
GHA_USER="${GHA_USER:-gha}"
RUNNER_VERSION="${RUNNER_VERSION:-2.317.0}"

# Hardening thresholds (the lessons): refuse below MIN_RAM, recommend REC_RAM,
# fail below MIN_DISK. Values in MiB / MiB / MiB respectively.
MIN_RAM_MIB=2048      # ~2 GB: below this `uv sync ... + reindex` OOMs.
REC_RAM_MIB=4096      # ~4 GB recommended for headroom.
MIN_DISK_MIB=5120     # ~5 GB free disk required.

log()  { printf '[provision-runner] %s\n' "$*"; }
warn() { printf '[provision-runner] WARN: %s\n' "$*" >&2; }
die()  { printf '[provision-runner] ERROR: %s\n' "$*" >&2; exit 1; }

usage() {
  sed -n '2,40p' "$0" | sed 's/^# \{0,1\}//'
}

# --- arg parsing -----------------------------------------------------------
while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --platform) PLATFORM="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
    --repo)     REPO_URL="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
    --token)    REG_TOKEN="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
    --labels)   RUNNER_LABELS="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
    --name)     RUNNER_NAME="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
    --user)     GHA_USER="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
    --runner-version) RUNNER_VERSION="${2:-}"; shift 2 ;;
    -h|--help)  usage; exit 0 ;;
    *) die "unknown argument: $1 (try --help)" ;;
  esac
done

[ -n "$PLATFORM" ] || die "--platform is required (github|gitlab)"
case "$PLATFORM" in
  github|gitlab) ;;
  *) die "--platform must be 'github' or 'gitlab' (got '$PLATFORM')" ;;
esac
[ -n "$REPO_URL" ] || die "--repo <url> is required"

if [ -n "$RUNNER_NAME" ]; then
  : "runner name supplied"
else
  RUNNER_NAME="$(hostname)-ai-techwriter"
fi

# Privilege: most steps need root (apt, swap, useradd, services). Re-exec under
# sudo when not already root, preserving the resolved config via env.
if [ "$(id -u)" -ne 0 ]; then
  if command -v sudo >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "re-executing under sudo for privileged steps"
    exec sudo -E \
      PLATFORM="$PLATFORM" REPO_URL="$REPO_URL" REG_TOKEN="$REG_TOKEN" \
      RUNNER_LABELS="$RUNNER_LABELS" RUNNER_NAME="$RUNNER_NAME" \
      GHA_USER="$GHA_USER" RUNNER_VERSION="$RUNNER_VERSION" \
      bash "$0"
  fi
  die "must run as root (or have sudo available)"
fi

# ===========================================================================
# 1. Prechecks (RAM + disk) — FAIL HARD before we touch anything.
# ===========================================================================
precheck_ram() {
  local mem_kib mem_mib
  if [ -r /proc/meminfo ]; then
    mem_kib="$(awk '/^MemTotal:/ {print $2}' /proc/meminfo)"
    mem_mib=$(( mem_kib / 1024 ))
  else
    warn "cannot read /proc/meminfo; skipping RAM precheck"
    return 0
  fi
  log "total RAM: ${mem_mib} MiB"
  if [ "$mem_mib" -lt "$MIN_RAM_MIB" ]; then
    die "only ${mem_mib} MiB RAM — below the ${MIN_RAM_MIB} MiB minimum. The agent job runs 'uv sync --extra dev --extra languages' + reindex and WILL OOM (we watched a ~961 MB box kill sshd mid-provision). Use a box with >= ${REC_RAM_MIB} MiB."
  fi
  if [ "$mem_mib" -lt "$REC_RAM_MIB" ]; then
    warn "${mem_mib} MiB RAM is below the recommended ${REC_RAM_MIB} MiB; relying on swap. An 8 GB / 4 CPU box is the proven-good size."
  fi
}

precheck_disk() {
  local free_mib
  free_mib="$(df -Pm / | awk 'NR==2 {print $4}')"
  log "free disk on /: ${free_mib} MiB"
  if [ "$free_mib" -lt "$MIN_DISK_MIB" ]; then
    die "only ${free_mib} MiB free disk on / — below the ${MIN_DISK_MIB} MiB minimum (runner + toolchain + checkout need headroom)."
  fi
}

precheck_ram
precheck_disk

# ===========================================================================
# 2. Swap insurance — BEFORE apt/build. FAIL HARD if it cannot be created.
#    (The 1 GB box OOM'd precisely because this had not run yet.)
# ===========================================================================
ensure_swap() {
  local cur_swap_kib swapfile size_mib
  cur_swap_kib="$(awk '/^SwapTotal:/ {print $2}' /proc/meminfo 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  if [ "${cur_swap_kib:-0}" -gt 0 ]; then
    log "swap already active ($(( cur_swap_kib / 1024 )) MiB) — leaving as-is"
    return 0
  fi
  swapfile="/swapfile"
  # Size swap at ~2x RAM, capped at 4096 MiB, floored at 2048 MiB.
  local mem_mib
  mem_mib="$(awk '/^MemTotal:/ {print int($2/1024)}' /proc/meminfo)"
  size_mib=$(( mem_mib * 2 ))
  [ "$size_mib" -gt 4096 ] && size_mib=4096
  [ "$size_mib" -lt 2048 ] && size_mib=2048
  log "creating ${size_mib} MiB swapfile at ${swapfile}"
  if [ ! -f "$swapfile" ]; then
    # fallocate is fast; dd is the portable fallback.
    if command -v fallocate >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      fallocate -l "${size_mib}M" "$swapfile" || rm -f "$swapfile"
    fi
    if [ ! -f "$swapfile" ]; then
      dd if=/dev/zero of="$swapfile" bs=1M count="$size_mib" status=none
    fi
  fi
  chmod 600 "$swapfile"
  mkswap "$swapfile" >/dev/null
  swapon "$swapfile"
  # Persist across reboots (idempotent — only append once).
  if ! grep -q "^${swapfile} " /etc/fstab 2>/dev/null; then
    printf '%s none swap sw 0 0\n' "$swapfile" >> /etc/fstab
  fi
  # Verify it actually took (do not silently continue into an OOM).
  cur_swap_kib="$(awk '/^SwapTotal:/ {print $2}' /proc/meminfo)"
  [ "${cur_swap_kib:-0}" -gt 0 ] || die "swapon reported success but SwapTotal is still 0 — refusing to continue into an OOM."
  log "swap active: $(( cur_swap_kib / 1024 )) MiB"
}

ensure_swap

# ===========================================================================
# 3. Base apt packages (critical — fail hard).
# ===========================================================================
install_base() {
  export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
  log "apt-get update + base packages"
  apt-get update -y
  apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    curl git jq tar ca-certificates build-essential
}

install_base

# ===========================================================================
# 4. gh CLI (best-effort — used to fetch a GitHub reg token, never required).
# ===========================================================================
install_gh() {
  if command -v gh >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "gh CLI already present"
    return 0
  fi
  log "installing gh CLI (best-effort)"
  {
    install -d -m 755 /etc/apt/keyrings
    curl -fsSL https://cli.github.com/packages/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg \
      | tee /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg >/dev/null
    chmod go+r /etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg
    printf 'deb [arch=%s signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/githubcli-archive-keyring.gpg] https://cli.github.com/packages stable main\n' \
      "$(dpkg --print-architecture)" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/github-cli.list
    apt-get update -y && apt-get install -y gh
  } || warn "gh CLI install failed (continuing — only needed to auto-fetch a GitHub reg token)"
}

# ===========================================================================
# 5. Non-root service user.
# ===========================================================================
ensure_user() {
  if id "$GHA_USER" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "service user '$GHA_USER' already exists"
  else
    log "creating service user '$GHA_USER'"
    useradd -m -s /bin/bash "$GHA_USER"
  fi
}

ensure_user
GHA_HOME="$(getent passwd "$GHA_USER" | cut -d: -f6)"
[ -n "$GHA_HOME" ] || die "could not resolve home dir for '$GHA_USER'"

# ===========================================================================
# 6. uv (best-effort) installed for the service user.
# ===========================================================================
install_uv() {
  if sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc 'command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1'; then
    log "uv already present for '$GHA_USER'"
    return 0
  fi
  log "installing uv for '$GHA_USER' (best-effort)"
  sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc \
    'curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh' \
    || warn "uv install failed (continuing — the CI job can install it too)"
}

# ===========================================================================
# 7. Goose (best-effort) — block/goose stable installer, symlinked onto PATH.
# ===========================================================================
install_goose() {
  if command -v goose >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "goose already present"
    return 0
  fi
  log "installing Goose (best-effort)"
  {
    sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc \
      'curl -fsSL https://github.com/block/goose/releases/download/stable/download_cli.sh | CONFIGURE=false bash'
    # Symlink the per-user binary onto the system PATH for the service.
    local goose_bin
    goose_bin="$(sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc 'command -v goose || echo "'"$GHA_HOME"'/.local/bin/goose"')"
    if [ -x "$goose_bin" ]; then
      ln -sf "$goose_bin" /usr/local/bin/goose
    fi
  } || warn "Goose install failed (continuing — verify + install manually if the agent job needs it)"
}

# ===========================================================================
# 8. beadloom + bd (best-effort, verified at the end).
# ===========================================================================
install_beadloom() {
  if sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc 'command -v beadloom >/dev/null 2>&1'; then
    log "beadloom already present for '$GHA_USER'"
    return 0
  fi
  log "installing beadloom for '$GHA_USER' (best-effort)"
  sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc \
    'command -v uv >/dev/null 2>&1 && uv tool install beadloom' \
    || warn "beadloom install failed (continuing — the CI job installs project deps itself)"
}

install_gh
install_uv
install_goose
install_beadloom

# ===========================================================================
# 9. Resolve a GitHub registration token via gh, if not supplied.
# ===========================================================================
resolve_github_token() {
  [ -n "$REG_TOKEN" ] && return 0
  command -v gh >/dev/null 2>&1 || die "no --token given and gh CLI unavailable to fetch one"
  log "fetching a GitHub registration token via gh"
  # Strip the scheme + host so we get OWNER/REPO for the API path.
  local slug
  slug="$(printf '%s' "$REPO_URL" | sed -E 's#^https?://[^/]+/##; s#\.git$##')"
  REG_TOKEN="$(gh api -X POST "repos/${slug}/actions/runners/registration-token" --jq '.token')"
  [ -n "$REG_TOKEN" ] || die "could not obtain a GitHub registration token"
}

# ===========================================================================
# 10. Register the runner — THE critical step (fail hard).
# ===========================================================================
register_github_runner() {
  resolve_github_token
  local arch rfile rdir
  case "$(uname -m)" in
    x86_64|amd64) arch="x64" ;;
    aarch64|arm64) arch="arm64" ;;
    *) die "unsupported arch for GitHub runner: $(uname -m)" ;;
  esac
  rdir="${GHA_HOME}/actions-runner"
  rfile="actions-runner-linux-${arch}-${RUNNER_VERSION}.tar.gz"
  install -d -o "$GHA_USER" -g "$GHA_USER" "$rdir"
  if [ ! -x "${rdir}/config.sh" ]; then
    log "downloading GitHub Actions runner ${RUNNER_VERSION} (${arch})"
    sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc "cd '$rdir' && curl -fsSLO 'https://github.com/actions/runner/releases/download/v${RUNNER_VERSION}/${rfile}' && tar xzf '${rfile}' && rm -f '${rfile}'"
  fi
  log "registering GitHub Actions runner (labels: ${RUNNER_LABELS})"
  sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc "cd '$rdir' && ./config.sh --url '$REPO_URL' --token '$REG_TOKEN' --labels '$RUNNER_LABELS' --name '$RUNNER_NAME' --unattended --replace"
  log "installing + starting the runner service"
  ( cd "$rdir" && ./svc.sh install "$GHA_USER" && ./svc.sh start )
}

register_gitlab_runner() {
  [ -n "$REG_TOKEN" ] || die "--token (GitLab project/runner token) is required for gitlab"
  if ! command -v gitlab-runner >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "installing gitlab-runner"
    curl -fsSL https://packages.gitlab.com/install/repositories/runner/gitlab-runner/script.deb.sh | bash
    apt-get install -y gitlab-runner
  fi
  log "registering GitLab Runner (tags: ${RUNNER_LABELS})"
  gitlab-runner register \
    --non-interactive \
    --url "$REPO_URL" \
    --registration-token "$REG_TOKEN" \
    --executor shell \
    --tag-list "$RUNNER_LABELS" \
    --description "$RUNNER_NAME" \
    --run-untagged="false" \
    --locked="true"
  log "ensuring gitlab-runner service is running"
  gitlab-runner start || systemctl restart gitlab-runner || true
}

case "$PLATFORM" in
  github) register_github_runner ;;
  gitlab) register_gitlab_runner ;;
esac

# ===========================================================================
# 11. Verify best-effort tools + report (do NOT abort the runner setup).
# ===========================================================================
report_tool() {
  local name="$1"; shift
  if "$@" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "OK: ${name} -> $("$@" 2>&1 | head -1)"
  else
    warn "MISSING/BROKEN: ${name} — install/verify it before the agent job runs"
  fi
}

log "=== verification report ==="
report_tool "goose" goose --version
report_tool "beadloom" sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc 'beadloom --version'
report_tool "bd" sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc 'bd version'
report_tool "uv" sudo -u "$GHA_USER" bash -lc 'uv --version'
log "=== done. The ${PLATFORM} runner '${RUNNER_NAME}' is registered (labels: ${RUNNER_LABELS}). ==="
log "If any best-effort tool above is MISSING, install it on this box; the runner registration itself succeeded."
